mnemonics.title: Virtual Base Constructor Order
mnemonics.content: Virtual constructors execute 'V'ery 'B'efore 'A'nything 'S'e - Virtual → Base → Sub/Derived. Remember V-B-S: Very Before Substantial

mnemonics.title: Diamond Problem Solution
mnemonics.content: Diamond = Two paths to same class. Virtual = One single copy. V = One; D = Duplicate gone. Virtual eliminates the Duplicate.

acronyms.usage: Pointer stored in derived class that points to the virtual base class subobject, enabling single instance access
Table used by compiler to manage virtual base class offsets in the inheritance hierarchy

acronyms.expansion: Ambiguity from multiple inheritance paths creating duplicate base class instances
acronyms.usage: Occurs when two classes inherit from same base and a third class inherits from both

visualTips.visualization: Draw a diamond shape: A at top (apex), B and C below (left and right), D at bottom. Arrows show inheritance direction. Without virtual: 2 paths from D to A. With virtual: 1 path to shared A.
Picture a class object as boxes. With virtual: D contains vbptr → points to shared A box. Without virtual: D contains two separate A boxes.
Think of Russian nesting dolls. The innermost (virtual base A) opens first, then B and C open, then D (outermost) opens last.

analogies.concept: Virtual base class as shared resource
analogies.analogy: Like a shared printer on a network - multiple departments (B, C) access the same printer (A) instead of each having their own.
Like a singleton pattern - ensures only one instance exists regardless of how many 'requests' (inheritance paths) come for it.
Like having one contact in your phone that multiple groups reference - one entry, many ways to find it.
